description: Letter from a man, in an unknown location, to his son Abū l-[Rabīʿ?] Sulaymān, in Fustat. To be delivered to the shop of Abū Naṣr al-Sharābī (same as in T-S AS 183.111 (PGPID 37875)?) in the Berber market (? sūq barbara). In Judaeo-Arabic, with the address in Arabic script. The handwriting is familiar but does not seem to be the handwriting of Abū Zikrī Kohen (who also had a son named Abū l-Rabīʿ Sulaymān). The letter is quite damaged. The sender complains about having sent eight previous letters and receiving no response. He has heard that his son is doing business in a shop. Either the sender or his wife is "perishing" (halaktu or halakat), presumably from anxiety over the lack of news. The addressee's mother is mentioned in the penultimate line. AA. ASE.
description_he: מכתב מאיש לבנו אבו אל-רביע סולימאן, בפוסטאט. בערבית-יהודית, עם הכתובת בכתב ערבי. המכתב ניזוק רבות. השולח מתלונן ששלח שמונה מכתבים קודמים ולא קיבל כל מענה. בנו עוסק במסחר בחנות. השולח או אשתו 'גוועים', כנראה מדאגה בשל העדר חדשות.
